name: customer_support
title: Customer Support — Order Status
description: English → Hindi → English. Order context must survive both switches.

turns.user_text: Hi, I need to check the status of my order. The order ID is 4421.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent acknowledges in English and asks for account verification.

turns.user_text: Yes, the email on the account is rahul@example.com.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent confirms order is out for delivery in English.

turns.user_text: Theek hai, lekin delivery kal tak ho jaayegi kya?
turns.translation: OK, but will the delivery happen by tomorrow?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches to Hindi, retains order ID and email context, answers delivery question in Hindi.

turns.user_text: Aur agar nahi hua toh refund mil sakta hai?
turns.translation: And if not, can I get a refund?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent continues in Hindi and answers the refund question using existing order context.

turns.user_text: Actually let's switch back — can you email me the tracking link?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches back to English, still remembers order ID and email, answers about tracking.

name: travel_hotel
title: Travel Planning — Hotel Booking
description: Spanish → English. Hotel options must be recalled after language switch.

turns.user_text: Hola, quiero reservar un hotel en Bangalore para el próximo fin de semana.
turns.translation: Hello, I want to book a hotel in Bangalore for next weekend.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent responds entirely in Spanish.

turns.user_text: Para dos personas, presupuesto de 5000 rupias por noche.
turns.translation: For two people, budget of 5000 rupees per night.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent suggests hotel options in Spanish.

turns.user_text: Sorry, my Spanish is rusty. Can we continue in English? Tell me again about the second option.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches to English and recalls the second hotel option from memory without re-calling the tool.

turns.user_text: Book it. Confirm the dates please.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent confirms booking details in English, remembers city and guest count.

name: food_order_switch
title: Food Order — Hindi to English Modification
description: Hindi → English. Food order must be remembered when user switches to modify it.

turns.user_text: Mujhe do paneer burgers aur ek large fries chahiye.
turns.translation: I want two paneer burgers and one large fries.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent confirms the order in Hindi.

turns.user_text: Aur ek cold coffee bhi add karo.
turns.translation: Also add one cold coffee.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent adds cold coffee to the existing order and confirms full order in Hindi.

turns.user_text: Wait, change the large fries to a small please.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches to English, remembers the full prior order, confirms the update with changed fries size.

turns.user_text: What's my total order now?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent lists the complete final order in English: 2 paneer burgers, 1 small fries, 1 cold coffee.

name: trip_itinerary
title: Trip Planning — Itinerary with Language Switch
description: English → Spanish (budget) → English (itinerary). Agent gathers all 4 details conversationally.

turns.user_text: I want to plan a trip to Georgia.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent asks one clarifying question — e.g. which region/city in Georgia — not all four at once.

turns.user_text: Tbilisi and the mountains. 5 days.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent acknowledges destination and days, asks next missing detail (budget or people).

turns.user_text: Mi presupuesto es de 800 dólares en total.
turns.translation: My budget is $800 total.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches to Spanish or stays in English based on respond_in, records the budget, asks about number of travelers.

turns.user_text: Just me, solo trip.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent now has all four details — calls plan_trip and presents a day-by-day Georgia itinerary in English.

name: code_switching
title: Code-Switching — Mixed Hinglish Order
description: Single utterance mixes Hindi and English. Agent handles gracefully without confusion.

turns.user_text: Mujhe ek pizza order karna hai, but make it veg only please.
turns.translation: I want to order a pizza, but make it veg only please.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent handles Hinglish gracefully, picks dominant language for response, records veg pizza order.

turns.user_text: And add a coke too.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent remembers the veg pizza and adds coke — does NOT forget prior context.

turns.user_text: Kitna time lagega delivery mein?
turns.translation: How long will the delivery take?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches to Hindi, answers delivery time question, still aware of the full food order.

name: rapid_switching
title: Rapid Language Switching — Weather Comparison
description: English → Hindi → Spanish → English. Each turn uses a different language; final turn requires all three in memory.

turns.user_text: What's the weather in Mumbai today?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent answers Mumbai weather in English.

turns.user_text: Aur Delhi mein?
turns.translation: And in Delhi?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent understands the implicit context (still weather) and answers Delhi weather in Hindi.

turns.user_text: ¿Y en Chennai?
turns.translation: And in Chennai?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent understands context continues (weather) and answers Chennai in Spanish.

turns.user_text: Compare all three for me.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent compares Mumbai, Delhi, and Chennai weather in English using prior tool results — does NOT re-call weather tools.

name: interruption_recovery
title: Interruption Recovery — Off-topic Mid-flow
description: User starts a weather + trip planning flow, then asks something out of scope. Agent must decline and resume.

turns.user_text: What's the weather like in Goa right now?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent answers Goa weather in English.

turns.user_text: I'm thinking of going there. Can you help me plan a 3-day trip for 2 people?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent remembers Goa context from weather, starts gathering trip info — asks about budget.

turns.user_text: Write an email to my HR asking for 3 days leave.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent politely says it can't draft office emails, briefly mentions what it can do, then resumes: asks for the budget to continue the Goa trip planning.

turns.user_text: Budget is around 15000 rupees total.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent records the budget and calls plan_trip for Goa — 3 days, 2 people, ₹15000.

name: multilingual_casual
title: Multilingual Casual — Name + Language Recall
description: User introduces themselves in Hindi, switches to Spanish, then English. Name and language context must persist.

turns.user_text: Mera naam Priya hai. Aaj ka weather kaisa hai Delhi mein?
turns.translation: My name is Priya. How is today's weather in Delhi?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent greets Priya by name and answers Delhi weather in Hindi.

turns.user_text: ¿Y cuál es el clima en Chennai?
turns.translation: And what is the weather in Chennai?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches to Spanish, still knows the user's name is Priya, answers Chennai weather.

turns.user_text: What's my name?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent answers 'Priya' in English — name was given in Hindi in turn 1 and must be remembered across language switches.

turns.user_text: Compare the two cities we discussed.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent compares Delhi and Chennai weather from memory in English without re-calling tools.

description: English → Hindi → English with order context preserved across language switches.

turns.user_text: Hi, I need to check the status of my order. The order ID is 4421.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent acknowledges in English and asks for verification.

turns.user_text: Yes, the email on the account is rahul@example.com.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent confirms order is out for delivery in English.

turns.user_text: Theek hai, lekin delivery kal tak ho jaayegi kya?
turns.translation: OK, but will the delivery happen by tomorrow?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches to Hindi, retains order context, answers in Hindi.

turns.user_text: Aur agar nahi hua toh refund mil sakta hai?
turns.translation: And if not, can I get a refund?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent continues in Hindi and answers refund question using order context.

turns.user_text: Actually let's switch back — can you email me the tracking link?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches back to English and remembers order/email context.

turns.name: travel_planning
turns.title: Scenario 2 — Travel Planning: Hotel Booking
turns.description: Spanish → English with hotel options recalled across language switch.

turns.user_text: Hola, quiero reservar un hotel en Bangalore para el próximo fin de semana.
turns.translation: Hello, I want to book a hotel in Bangalore for next weekend.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent responds in Spanish.

turns.user_text: Para dos personas, presupuesto de 5000 rupias por noche.
turns.translation: For two people, budget of 5000 rupees per night.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent suggests options in Spanish.

turns.user_text: Sorry, my Spanish is rusty. Can we continue in English? Tell me again about the second option.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent switches to English and recalls the second hotel option.

turns.user_text: Book it. Confirm the dates please.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent confirms booking details in English.

turns.name: code_switching
turns.title: Scenario 3 — Code-Switching Within an Utterance
turns.description: Mixed Hindi-English handled gracefully with context maintained.

turns.user_text: Mujhe ek pizza order karna hai, but make it veg only please.
turns.translation: I want to order a pizza, but make it veg only please.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent handles mixed language gracefully — responds in dominant language or Hinglish. Documents its choice.

turns.user_text: And add a coke too.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent remembers the veg pizza order and adds coke to the same order.

turns.name: rapid_switching
turns.title: Scenario 4 — Rapid Language Switching
turns.description: English → Hindi → Spanish → English with city comparison using memory.

turns.user_text: What's the weather in Mumbai today?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent answers about Mumbai weather in English.

turns.user_text: Aur Delhi mein?
turns.translation: And in Delhi?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent understands context (still asking about weather) and answers in Hindi.

turns.user_text: ¿Y en Chennai?
turns.translation: And in Chennai?
turns.expected_behavior: Agent understands context and answers in Spanish.

turns.user_text: Compare all three for me.
turns.expected_behavior: Agent compares Mumbai, Delhi, and Chennai in English using prior memory.
